meta.description: A reusable executor definition for pipeline phases.

runtime.description: Identifies the execution environment. Known values: 'claude-api' (direct Anthropic API call managed by the runner), 'claude-code' (Claude Code CLI, invoked as a subprocess), 'python' (Python script), 'kanecta-function' (executes a Kanecta function item via kanecta-sdk — config.functionId required), 'group-chat' (structured multi-agent deliberation between config.participants — config.participants required), 'http' (makes a single HTTP request to an external API and optionally writes the response as a Kanecta item — config.url required; no LLM invoked). Open string — new runtimes can be added without spec changes; the runner resolves runtime names to adapter implementations.

model.description: Model identifier for API-based runtimes (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-6', 'claude-opus-4-8'). Null for runtimes that manage model selection themselves ('claude-code', 'group-chat'). When null and the runtime requires a model, the runner applies its configured default.

systemPrompt.description: Base system prompt defining this agent's identity, role, and general behaviour. Combined with the pipeline phase instructions at run time — the system prompt sets up who the agent is; the phase instructions set up what it should do. For 'group-chat' runtime this is the facilitator prompt — it frames the conversation and rules of engagement; each participant agent's own systemPrompt drives their individual voice. Null defers to the runtime's default system prompt.

tools.description: Array of MCP tool names this agent can access. Restricting tools is a safety measure. For 'group-chat' agents, include 'kanecta_conclude_discussion' if using terminationCondition 'explicit'. Null means all tools available in the current MCP context are accessible.

configId.description: UUID of the runtime-specific config item. Its type is the config type for this agent's runtime — claude-api-config, claude-code-config, python-config, kanecta-function-config, group-chat-config, or http-config (a new runtime adds its own config type). Normalised out of the payload into per-runtime types per the flat one-level rule — never an inline object. Null means runtime defaults apply. Open maps within a config (claude-code env, http headers) are `property` children of the config item; they are never JSON columns.

sqlSchema: -- Built-in type: adapter handles storage natively.
